explanation: The requirement means that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This is expressed by stating globally (G) that if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true.

The phrase 'while prop_0, then all of the following conditions hold: prop_1' means 'whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 is also true at the same time'. This is best expressed as: globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 also holds.

The phrase 'While prop_0, then all of the following conditions hold: prop_1' means that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true, for the entire duration that prop_0 holds. This is a safety property, often expressed as: globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must hold as well.
The statement means that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This is globally true, i.e., at any moment if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must be true as well.

The requirement is that while prop_0 holds (i.e., in every state where prop_0 is true), then prop_1 must hold. In LTL, this is expressed as: globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true.
The requirement is that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This is expressed by stating that it is always the case (G) that if prop_0, then also prop_1.
Whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This can be written as 'G(prop_0 -> prop_1)': globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must hold.
While prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold. That is, at every moment where prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true. This is expressed in LTL as globally, if prop_0 then prop_1.

The statement says that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This is a safety property and can be captured by a global implication: globally, if prop_0 holds then prop_1 must also hold.

The statement requires that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time, for as long as prop_0 is true. This is equivalent to stating that globally (everywhere), if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 is also true.
